frameworks.name: Bayesian probabilistic modeling
frameworks.one_line: Computational theories act as constrained prior distributions on neural data.

frameworks.name: bottom-up statistical pattern search
frameworks.one_line: Data-driven analysis of large-scale neural recordings.

frameworks.name: top-down computational theory
frameworks.one_line: Theory-first approach considering plausible neural implementations.

frameworks.name: temporal difference learning
frameworks.one_line: Worked example linking dopamine theory to neural data via probabilistic model.

core_question: How can computational theory and statistical analysis of neural recordings be more intimately linked for data-driven theory revision?
key_assumptions: Computational theories impose prior structure on plausible neural data
Bottom-up and top-down approaches are complementary, not opposed
Probabilistic models can mediate theory-observation linkage
key_findings: Recent literature exemplifies a theory-driven pipeline for neural data analysis
TD learning model of dopamine illustrates the theory-constrained statistical approach
interpretations: Probabilistic models that embed computational theory enable rigorous, data-driven theory testing and revision
Bayesian framing reconciles bottom-up and top-down computational neuroscience
